articles.title: Why Go is Winning the Backend Wars
articles.why: Solid case for Go if you're picking a backend language
articles.description: Simple concurrency, fast compilation, and a robust standard library — Go's boring reputation is its biggest asset.

articles.title: The Case Against ORMs
articles.why: Will make you rethink your data layer assumptions
articles.description: After years of ActiveRecord and Hibernate, one veteran backend developer makes the argument for raw SQL.

articles.title: Cloudflare Workers vs Lambda: A Real Comparison
articles.why: Saves you hours of research if you're choosing a serverless platform
articles.description: Cold starts, pricing, DX, and ecosystem — a comprehensive head-to-head for serverless compute in 2026.

articles.title: How Figma Scales WebSockets to Millions of Connections
articles.why: Real engineering detail you won't find in a blog post
articles.description: Engineering post from the Figma team on multiplexing, state sync, and surviving network partitions in real-time apps.

articles.title: Garbage Collection Tuning in the JVM — A Field Guide
articles.why: Essential if you're running Java in production and seeing pauses
articles.description: G1GC vs ZGC vs Shenandoah: when to use each, and the flags that actually move the needle in production.

repos.name: vercel/next.js
repos.why: Still the default choice for React in production — know what you're picking
repos.description: The React Framework for the Web — server components, app router, and edge-ready deployments.

repos.name: biomejs/biome
repos.why: Drops ESLint and Prettier in one faster tool — worth the migration
repos.description: One toolchain for your web project — fast formatter and linter written in Rust.

repos.name: ollama/ollama
repos.why: Easiest way to run open-source LLMs locally with zero setup
repos.description: Get up and running with Llama 3, Mistral, Gemma, and other large language models locally.

repos.name: electric-sql/electric
repos.why: Local-first sync on Postgres — solves offline and real-time in one layer
repos.description: Sync engine for building local-first software on top of Postgres.

videos.title: Stop Writing Boilerplate: AI-Assisted Refactoring in 2026
videos.why: Live session showing exactly what AI tools can and can't do in a real codebase
videos.description: A live coding session showing how modern AI coding tools have changed the daily workflow of a senior developer — what they're actually good at, and what still requires a human brain.

videos.title: Htmx 3.0 — Is the Hype Justified?
videos.why: Hear it straight from the creator before forming an opinion
videos.description: Carson Gross demos the new features in htmx 3.0 and debates whether hypermedia-first is viable for complex apps.

videos.title: Turso + LibSQL: SQLite for the Edge
videos.why: Best explainer of distributed SQLite you'll find right now
videos.description: How Turso brings globally distributed SQLite replicas to edge functions with sub-millisecond reads.

videos.title: Writing Your Own Linux Container from Scratch
videos.why: Demystifies Docker internals in a way docs never will
videos.description: Understanding namespaces, cgroups, and overlayfs by building a container runtime in 200 lines of Go.

podcasts.title: Soft Skills Engineering — Ep. 410: Managing Up Without Being a Sycophant
podcasts.why: Tactical advice on a skill that separates good engineers from great ones
podcasts.description: Dave and Jamison discuss how to influence your manager and advocate for yourself without coming across as political.

podcasts.title: Soft Skills Engineering — Ep. 409: The Art of the Code Review
podcasts.why: Concrete language and mindset shifts for giving better reviews
podcasts.description: How to give feedback that improves code quality without damaging team relationships — and how to receive it gracefully.

kelvinsPick.title: Explainshell — The Best Way to Understand Shell Commands
kelvinsPick.description: Paste any shell command and explainshell breaks it down piece by piece, pulling descriptions directly from man pages. I've been using this for a decade and I still reach for it weekly. Essential bookmarked tool.
